creator: Sarah Perez
title: (No AI) Reid Hoffman weighs in on the ‘tokenmaxxing’ debate
dc:creator: Sarah Perez
content: Reid Hoffman says tracking AI token use can gauge adoption, but cautions it should be paired with context and not treated as a direct productivity metric.

creator: Ivan Mehta
title: (No AI) Adobe’s new Firefly AI assistant can use Creative Cloud apps to complete tasks
dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
content: Adobe says the assistant can work across apps like Firefly, Photoshop, Premiere, Lightroom, Express, Illustrator and its other apps to do tasks for you.

title: (No AI) Adobe embraces conversational AI editing, marking a ‘fundamental shift’ in creative work
author: Jess Weatherbed
content:
![A screenshot of the Firefly AI Assistant editing a headshot.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/04/Adobe-Firefly-Innovations-Visual-Asset.png?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

You don’t need to understand any fancy editing terms — just describe what changes you want to make. | Image: Adobe

Adobe is fully embracing AI tools that enable creators to edit their work using descriptive prompts, instead of manually using specific Creative Cloud apps. The software giant's new Firefly AI Assistant allows users to describe what they want to change by typing their own words into a conversational interface.

Adobe says this marks a "fundamental shift in how creative work is done" by removing skill barriers and laborious tasks, while still giving creatives full control over their work. It'll be "available soon" on the Firefly AI studio platform according to Adobe, though no specific launch date was provided in the announcement.

The unifi …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/912287/adobe-firefly-ai-assistant-announcement-editing)

title: (No AI) Grok’s sexual deepfakes almost got it banned from Apple’s App Store. Almost.
author: Robert Hart
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/04/STK468_APPLE_ANTITRUST_CVIRGINIA_G.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Apple quietly threatened to kick Elon Musk's AI app, Grok, from its App Store in January over its failure to curb the surge of nonconsensual sexual deepfakes flooding X, [according](https://www.nbcnews.com/tech/tech-news/apple-threat-remove-grok-app-store-deepfake-letter-musk-x-ai-rcna331677) to _NBC News_. It was a muted show of force from one of tech's most powerful gatekeepers, made behind closed doors even as the [undressing crisis unfolded in full public view](https://www.theverge.com/news/859715/x-grok-ai-deepfakes) and [criticism over Apple's cowardice](https://www.theverge.com/news/862460/apple-google-app-stores-ditch-grok-x-open-letters) mounted.

In a letter [obtained](https://www.nbcnews.com/tech/tech-news/apple-threat-remove-grok-app-store-deepfake-letter-musk-x-ai-rcna331677) by _NBC News_, Apple told US senators it "contacted the teams behind both X and Grok after it received complaints and saw news coverage of the scandal" and demanded that the developers "create a plan to improve content moderation." …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/912297/apple-app-store-ban-grok-x-deepfakes)
contentSnippet:
Apple quietly threatened to kick Elon Musk's AI app, Grok, from its App Store in January over its failure to curb the surge of nonconsensual sexual deepfakes flooding X, according to NBC News. It was a muted show of force from one of tech's most powerful gatekeepers, made behind closed doors even as the undressing crisis unfolded in full public view and criticism over Apple's cowardice mounted.
In a letter obtained by NBC News, Apple told US senators it "contacted the teams behind both X and Grok after it received complaints and saw news coverage of the scandal" and demanded that the developers "create a plan to improve content moderation." …
Read the full story at The Verge.

It was a muted show of force from one of tech's most powerful gatekeepers, made behind closed doors even as the undressing […]

creator: Kate Knibbs
title: (No AI) AI Slop Is Making the Internet Fake-Happy

content: A new study examines the impact of the rise of AI-generated websites on the internet—and found some surprising results.

creator: Matt Burgess
title: (No AI) The Deepfake Nudes Crisis in Schools Is Much Worse Than You Thought
dc:creator: Matt Burgess
content: An analysis by WIRED and Indicator found nearly 90 schools and 600 students around the world impacted by AI-generated deepfake nude images—and the problem shows no signs of going away.
contentSnippet: An analysis by WIRED and Indicator found nearly 90 schools and 600 students around the world impacted by AI-generated deepfake nude images—and the problem shows no signs of going away.
